name: Pygame

performance.notes: SDL2-based; Python overhead limits complex games; fine for 2D at 60fps

agent_readiness.notes: Python 2D game development library built on SDL2. Sprite system, collision detection, sound, keyboard/mouse/joystick input, display management. Excellent for learning game development in Python. Large community, tons of tutorials. AI simulation and RL environment prototyping. pygame-ce (Community Edition) is the maintained fork with better performance. Limited to 2D — use Ursina or Panda3D for 3D in Python.

gotchas: Python GIL limits performance for complex games
Original pygame less maintained — prefer pygame-ce (community edition)
No built-in scene management or UI framework
best_when: Simple games, tools, and interactive visualizations
avoid_when: When performance is critical (GIL limits Python)
summary: Pygame is the classic Python 2D game library built on SDL2. Best for learning game development, rapid prototyping, and RL simulation environments. Large community, excellent tutorials. Use pygame-ce (community edition) for better performance over original pygame. Limited to 2D — consider Godot for production games or more complex needs.
